item.title: How to Contribute
item.description: 👉 Make sure to read the Code of Conduct Contribute to code 👉 The Doks code lives in the `doks` repository. Create an issue Bug report Feature request Create a Pull Request 👉 Contact the maintainer before proposing any significant changes. Follow the GitHub flow. Follow the Conventional Commits Specification Improve documentation 👉 The documentation lives in `./content/docs/` of the `getdoks.org` repository. Follow the GitHub flow. Follow the Conventional Commits Specification Create an issue Bug report Feature request Help others Doks Discussions is the place to get help and help others with Doks.

item.title: Financial Contributions
item.description: We use Open Collective to receive contributions, share our budget, and manage expenses. Visit the Doks Open Collective page. Become a Sponsor Become a sponsor starting at $100.00 per month. Your logo — with a link to your website — will show up on the Doks website, the Doks repository, and the Doks Open Collective page. Become a Backer Become a backer starting at $5.00 per month. Your avatar will show up on the Doks repository and the Doks Open Collective page.

item.title: Code of Conduct
item.description: Our Pledge In the interest of fostering an open and welcoming environment, we as contributors and maintainers pledge to making participation in our project and our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and orientation. Our Standards Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:
